description: Soft soothing bedtime lullaby with minimal arrangement to help children fall asleep
suno_tags: gentle lullaby, soft female vocals, music box, acoustic guitar, soothing, dreamy, minimal, 65 BPM
vocal_style: Soft gentle adult female voice, hushed and warm, like a mother singing at bedside, breathy and tender

tips: Use soft imagery: stars, moon, clouds, blankets, warmth
Slow pacing with gentle rhythm — no sudden energy shifts
Repetition is key — repeat the chorus exactly for calming effect
Use lulling vowel sounds: oo, ah, oh, ee
End lines with soft consonants, avoid hard stops

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]

[Verse 1]
[Soft]
Close your eyes, my little one
The day is done, the stars have come
The moon is bright, the world is still
Rest your head upon the hill

[Chorus]
Sleep now, sleep now, safe and sound
Love is here all around
Dream away till morning light
Sleep now, sleep now, goodnight

[Verse 2]
[Soft]
Blankets warm and pillows deep
Gentle winds will rock your sleep
Owls are singing lullabies
Underneath the velvet skies

[Chorus]
Sleep now, sleep now, safe and sound
Love is here all around
Dream away till morning light
Sleep now, sleep now, goodnight

[Verse 3]
[Whispered]
I'll be here when you wake up
With a smile and a loving cup
But for now just close your eyes
And let the night sing lullabies

[Chorus]
[Soft]
Sleep now, sleep now, safe and sound
Love is here all around
Dream away till morning light
Sleep now, sleep now, goodnight

[Outro]
[Whispered]
Goodnight... goodnight...
[End]
do: Keep energy flat and low throughout — no builds or climaxes
Repeat the chorus identically for hypnotic calming effect
Use nature imagery that evokes safety and warmth
End with a whispered outro that trails off
dont: Never use children's choir or kids singing in suno_tags
Avoid any upbeat or energetic moments
Don't use loud instruments like drums or electric guitar
Avoid themes of separation, loneliness, or fear
Don't change tempo or dynamics suddenly
